Notes / explanation:
- The visit returned a content value of "https://support.github.com?tags=dotcom-footer" instead of the repository HTML that normally contains the requested facts (language, stars, license, description). Because of that, the page content needed to extract the requested fields was not available in the visit result.

None of those links, as provided in the visit result, explicitly contained the repository metadata (language, stars, license, description).

Citations (URLs used as evidence in this extraction):

- Retrieved content value from visit: https://support.github.com?tags=dotcom-footer
- Links list (see ordered links above): URLs 1..20 as listed above

If you want, I can:

- If you can provide the repository page HTML or a screenshot of the repository header, I can extract the four requested facts directly from that content.

details.intent: Review the extracted facts for internal consistency, check if any item is missing or ambiguous, and plan corrective steps if needed (e.g., re-visit specific links such as LICENSE file).
checks: If license label on the repo page is ambiguous, plan a follow-up visit to the LICENSE file link found on the repo.
If star count is shown in shorthand without exact number and the user requires a precise figure, note that and plan to extract the exact count from the hover/tooltip or API.
If primary language is not shown or multiple languages are listed, decide which one to record as 'primary' based on the largest percentage indicator on the page.
expected_outcomes: Either confirm facts are complete and unambiguous, or identify specific follow-up visit nodes required (with exact URLs copied from the visit response).
details.rationale: Ensures high-quality, verifiable output and prepares corrective actions if the initial visit produced ambiguous data. This is a post-action insight step as required by the workflow rules.

a31283bb-1ab2-4a3e-a516-4dfa91fadab9.title: Visit the CPython GitHub repository page

details.intent: Directly load the repository page requested by the user and extract the specified facts from the live page (do not rely on search results).

extraction_plan.primary_programming_language: Locate the language badge/summary on the repo page (usually in the top-right or in the repository metadata) and record the primary language shown.
extraction_plan.star_count_approx: Locate the star button/indicator near the top of the page and capture the displayed star count; if exact number is shown, record it and also create an approximate rounded value (e.g., '~Xk').
extraction_plan.license_type: Find the license label or file reference shown on the repository page (often in the repo metadata or as a link to LICENSE) and capture the license name.
extraction_plan.repository_description: Extract the short description text immediately under the repository name/header that describes what the repository is.

verification_steps: Record the exact text snippets or HTML elements where each fact was found (for provenance).
If a license link is present, capture the link URL and the license label text.
If star count uses shorthand (e.g., '48k'), capture both the shorthand and any tooltip/exact number if available on the page.
Timestamp the page access to show when the data was observed.
details.rationale: The user explicitly required visiting the GitHub page directly. This visit node fetches the live repository page and extracts verifiable, page-sourced facts (language, stars, license, description). Direct visit avoids reliance on search results and meets the mandate.
details.notes: The system will also make an automatic vector DB query as part of the node execution (per framework rules) to retrieve any stored context; primary data will be taken from the visited page.
